divergence: The last glacier was bought at auction by a beverage conglomerate
story_angle: consumer report reviewing the apocalypse's amenities, with star rating
headline: Glacier Classic™ Launch Draws Three-Mile Queue; Critics Praise 'Notes of Mammoth' in Limited-Edition Ice
deck: Scalpers want forty bullets a cube. The Conglomerate calls this "organic community pricing."
article:
MELT SITE ONE — The queue formed Tuesday. By Thursday's launch it stretched three miles across the salt flats, shimmering with heat and brand loyalty, as the Hydra-Cola Conglomerate released Glacier Classic™: genuine ice from Earth's last glacier, sold by the cube, while supplies last. Supplies will not last. That is the entire product.

The launch event set a new standard for wasteland hospitality. Drones drop-seeded the crowd with coupons redeemable against future thirst. A brand ambassador in fur-lined hazmat gear performed the ceremonial First Chip, and the amplified crack of calving ice rolled over the flats like weather used to. Two stars for shade availability. Five stars for spectacle. The Conglomerate has asked us to average this to four; we have, because they hold the water.

The tasting notes hold up. Crisp. Mineral. Ten thousand years of compressed winter with a long, faintly apocalyptic finish — this reviewer detected pine, woodsmoke, and what the sommelier card identifies as 'notes of mammoth.' Vice President of Scarcity Operations Greta Voll explained the pricing model from a refrigerated podium: "Scarcity is our most renewable resource. When the glacier is gone, the brand remains. You can't drink a brand, but you can belong to one, and belonging is hydration for the soul." The crowd, dangerously dehydrated, applauded.

The loyalty program launched alongside: drink ten thousand cubes and receive a commemorative photograph of the glacier as it appeared before you drank it. Redemption is expected to be low. "That's the beauty of it," Voll said.

At the queue's end, Marla Krenn, 44, of the Rustline settlements, waited with her family's canteen and both her buckets. "It's not about the water," she said, watching the ice sparkle behind blast glass. "It's about being part of something before it's gone forever." She paused. "Also the water."
author: Rust Calloway, Consumer Survival Desk

oped.title: They Called Me Mad for Investing in Humidity
oped.author: Baron Flint, Moisture Futures Pioneer
oped.body: When I pitched dew rights in '68, the settlement council laughed me out of the bunker. Who's laughing now? Still them, technically, but the laughter is drier. The Conglomerate's glacier play proves what I've preached for decades: everything the old world left lying around is inventory. Air. Shade. The concept of morning. My proposal before the council this session is modest — privatize dew, meter it at the blade of grass, and let the market decide who wakes up damp. The whiners say this is 'the exact thing that ended the world.' The world ended because of amateurs. I am not an amateur. I am a man whose rain-barrel venture was destroyed by predatory undercutting from the sky itself, and I have never forgotten it, and this time I will be paid.

classifieds.text: Souvenir cube, Glacier Classic™, partially licked. Provenance available. Sentiment negotiable.
Queue-standers wanted for future drops. Pay: your place in the queue. References checked, hydration not provided.
LOST: canteen. Sentimental value, actual value, all remaining value. Keep the canteen. Return the water.
The Conglomerate reminds residents that catching rain constitutes piracy of an upcoming product.

panels.caption: The vendor unveils the sacred cube. The crowd gasps. One man weeps.
'His tears!' cries the vendor. 'Somebody collect those!'
By dusk there's a new product: Grief, Lightly Salted™.

joke.setup: How many wastelanders does it take to open a bottle of Glacier Classic?
joke.punchline: Nobody knows. No one's ever afforded the twist-off tier.
joke.text: How many wastelanders does it take to open a bottle of Glacier Classic? Nobody knows. No one's ever afforded the twist-off tier.

sponsor_ads.headline: Glacier Classic™
sponsor_ads.body: Ten thousand years in the making. Gone by Thursday.

sponsor_ads.headline: DustDew Home Stills
sponsor_ads.body: Turn your tears into a beverage-adjacent liquid the whole family can dispute.

sponsor_ads.headline: The Moisture Church
sponsor_ads.body: Sunday services at a merciful 40% humidity. Tithing in sweat accepted.

sponsor_ads.headline: Bullet Bank & Trust
sponsor_ads.body: Water-backed securities for the liquid-minded investor.

real_world_echo: Climate anxiety meets sneaker-drop scarcity marketing and luxury water brands — the last glacier sold as a limited-edition collectible.

stories.headline: The Puddle of Sector 9, Beloved by All, Evaporates at 34 (Hours)
stories.deck: It is survived by a patch of mud and the seventeen families who knew it best.
stories.article:
The Puddle of Sector 9, the region's most distinguished standing water since the June rain, evaporated peacefully Tuesday afternoon, aged thirty-four hours. In its brief life the Puddle sustained seventeen families, one dog of uncertain allegiance, and the settlement's first mirror-viewing in a decade, during which several residents learned what they look like and asked for the water back.

It is survived by a patch of mud, already contested. Services will be held at dawn, in the shade, quickly. In lieu of flowers, the family requests rain, from anyone.

stories.headline: Scalper Union Demands Recognition, Water, and a Cooler With Its Name On It
stories.deck: The Conglomerate responds by declaring them 'unauthorized retail partners,' then franchising them.
stories.article:
The Amalgamated Scalpers of Melt Site One filed for recognition Thursday, demanding collective bargaining rights, shade, and one commemorative cooler. The union's forty members control an estimated 60 percent of secondhand Glacier Classic™ cubes, most of them now trading as 'Glacier Classic-Adjacent Moisture.'

The Conglomerate initially declared the scalpers unauthorized retail partners, a designation carrying penalties. By afternoon it had franchised them instead, a designation carrying fees. Union leadership called this a total victory, quietly, from inside the new branded vests they are contractually required to purchase.
